segments.text: I have made a covenant with my eyes. How then could I gaze with desire at a virgin?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For what is the allotment of God from above, or the heritage from the Almighty on high?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Does not disaster come to the unjust and calamity to the workers of iniquity?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Does He not see my ways and count my every step?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I have walked in falsehoodor my foot has rushed to deceit,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: let God weigh me with honest scales, that He may know my integrity.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
If my steps have turned from the path, if my heart has followed my eyes,
or if impurity has stuck to my hands,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then may another eat what I have sown, and may my crops be uprooted.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If my heart has been enticed by my neighbor’s wife, or I have lurked at his door,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then may my own wife grind grain for another, and may other men sleep with her.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For that would be a heinous crime, an iniquity to be judged.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For it is a fire that burns down to Abaddon; it would root out my entire harvest.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I have rejected the cause of my manservant or maidservantwhen they made a complaint against me,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: what will I do when God rises to judge? How will I answer when called to account?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Did not He who made me in the womb also make them? Did not the same One form us in the womb?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I have denied the desires of the pooror allowed the widow’s eyes to fail,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have eaten my morsel alone, not sharing it with the fatherless—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: though from my youth I reared him as would a father, and from my mother’s womb I guided the widow—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have seen one perish for lack of clothing, or a needy man without a cloak,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if his heart has not blessed me for warming him with the fleece of my sheep,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have lifted up my hand against the fatherlessbecause I saw that I had support in the gate,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then may my arm fall from my shoulder and be torn from its socket.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For calamity from God terrifies me, and His splendor I cannot overpower.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If I have put my trust in goldor called pure gold my security,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have rejoiced in my great wealthbecause my hand had gained so much,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have beheld the sun in its radianceor the moon moving in splendor,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: so that my heart was secretly enticed and my hand threw a kiss from my mouth,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: this would also be an iniquity to be judged, for I would have denied God on high.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If I have rejoiced in my enemy’s ruin, or exulted when evil befell him—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have not allowed my mouth to sinby asking for his life with a curse—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if the men of my house have not said,‘Who is there who has not had his fill?’—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: but no stranger had to lodge on the street, for my door has been open to the traveler—
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: if I have covered my transgressions like Adam by hiding my guilt in my heart,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: because I greatly feared the crowds and the contempt of the clans terrified me, so that I kept silent and would not go outside—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: (Oh, that I had one to hear me! Here is my signature. Let the Almighty answer me; let my accuser compose an indictment.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder and wear it like a crown.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would give account of all my steps; I would approach Him like a prince.)—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if my land cries out against me and its furrows weep together,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have devoured its produce without paymentor broken the spirit of its tenants,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then let briers grow instead of wheat and stinkweed instead of barley.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Thus conclude the words of Job.
